title: Desmodiastrum belgaumense
taxonomy: Desmodiastrum belgaumense is classified within the family Fabaceae, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as legumes. It is further grouped into the subfamily Faboideae. The genus Desmodiastrum is a monotypic genus, consisting of only one species, D. belgaumense. This plant is native to India and is found in the Western Ghats region. The exact classification of Desmodiastrum belgaumense is further refined to the tribe Desmodieae.
uses: Desmodiastrum belgaumense is reported to have various medicinal uses, including its potential in treating fever, cough, and other respiratory issues. Its leaves are also used in traditional medicine to treat skin conditions and wounds. In addition, the plant is believed to have antimicrobial properties, making it useful in fighting bacterial and fungal infections. The plant's roots are also used in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues and reduce inflammation. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Desmodiastrum belgaumense for medicinal purposes.
